summary: Jeff Atwood shares his experience customizing the Windows XP boot screen as part of a workplace MAME arcade cabinet project. He recommends StarDock's free BootSkin utility for easily swapping boot screens, noting the technical limitation of 16 colors at 640x480 resolution. Finding an existing Windows 1.01 bootskin poorly executed, he created and shares his own pixel-perfect version. The post exemplifies the tinkerer spirit of early 2000s PC customization culture.
key_points: BootSkin is a free utility that makes Windows XP boot screen customization accessible to non-experts
The XP boot process has a hard technical limitation of 16 colors at 640x480 resolution
Testing system-level changes in Virtual PC or VMware first is a sensible precaution
The author's attention to detail led him to fix a poorly-scaled community contribution rather than accept mediocrity
TweakUI PowerToy enables automatic login, useful for dedicated-purpose machines like arcade cabinets
money_quotes: Thankfully, that's 16 colors of your choice, not the classic default 16 'Windows' colors.
The author didn't do a good job of scaling the image, so it looked horrible. I reformatted it so it's pixel perfect.
If you're worried about the effects of this boot screen change, try it in a Virtual PC or VMWare image first.
key_insight: Even small customization projects benefit from attention to detail and a willingness to fix what others have done poorly.
